prompt:
Develop a NutriOps Offline Operations & Meal Planning system for field teams and consumers who need reliable, on-device nutrition planning and service operations without any network dependency. The app supports three roles: Administrator (configures rules and content), Agent (handles exceptions and audits), and End User (follows plans and receives reminders). End Users create a profile (age range, dietary pattern, allergies, goal such as "lose 1 lb/week," preferred meal times) and receive a weekly plan with a daily calorie budget and macro targets; each recommendation must show at least two explainable reasons such as "fits 1,900 kcal/day budget" and "low-sodium preference." Meals allow one-click swaps to equivalent portions within ±10% calories and ±5 g protein, and the app prevents plans with an end date earlier than the start date or a study/meal frequency exceeding 7 days/week. The Learning Plan lifecycle is modeled as not started, in progress, paused, completed, and archived; status changes require confirmations and enforce that completed plans cannot be edited unless duplicated. An Operations & Configuration Center lets Administrators configure homepage modules and ad slots (local-only placements), define campaigns/landing topics, coupons and promotions (e.g., "$5 off when weekly adherence ≥ 5 days"), and enforce black/whitelist and purchase-limit policies (e.g., max 2 coupons per user per 30 days), all with config versioning and canary rollout to a chosen 10% of local users on the device profile. A Metrics and Threshold Rules Engine evaluates adherence, exceptions, and operational KPIs with hysteresis (enter at 80%, exit at 90%), minimum duration (condition must hold 10 minutes), compound conditions (A AND (B OR C)), effective time windows, rule versioning, and the ability to back-calculate outcomes for any date range stored on-device. Exception and after-sales handling provides a ticket flow for delay/dispute/lost-item with SLA clocks (first response within 4 business hours, resolution target 3 days), evidence upload (image/text only), automated compensation suggestions (e.g., $3–$20) requiring Agent approval above $10, and an immutable audit trail. A Messaging and To-Do Center delivers in-app messages only (email/SMS/WeCom disabled in offline mode), triggered by events like plan started, ticket updated, or rule rollout, with templates, variables, quiet hours (9:00 PM–7:00 AM), and reminder frequency capped at 3 per day.

The Android app is built with Jetpack Compose for a responsive, tablet/phone-friendly UI, Hilt for dependency injection across ViewModel and UseCase layers, and SQLDelight for an encrypted local SQLite database; no server-side logic, remote APIs, or cloud storage are permitted. Core data models include Users, Profiles, MealPlans, Meals, SwapMappings, LearningPlans, Rules (with versions and effective windows), Rollouts, MetricsSnapshots, Tickets, EvidenceItems (stored as scoped-storage URIs), Orders, ChargingSessions, and AuditEvents; all writes that affect billing/order/ticket state must use SQL transactions and maintain reconciliation state transitions with timestamped actor IDs. Queries must be indexed (composite indexes such as (userId, date) for plans and (status, updatedAt) for tickets) to keep typical lookups under 50 ms for up to 1,000,000 rows on mid-range devices. Lists use RecyclerView + DiffUtil where legacy views are required and Compose lazy lists elsewhere, targeting 60 fps scrolling; images are downsampled on load with an LRU cache capped at 20 MB to prevent OOM. All rule evaluation, back-calculation, and evidence processing runs off the main thread using Kotlin coroutines with IO/Default dispatchers to avoid ANR, and any deferred reminders use WorkManager scheduled only when the device is idle to reduce battery impact while still functioning fully offline under Android 10+ scoped storage and background execution limits. Sensitive fields (password hashes, compensation amounts, notes) are encrypted at rest, and UI masks ticket PII by default with an Agent-only reveal toggle logged to the audit trail.
